name: Jay Inslee
subject: We need to remove Donald Trump from office.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
body:
| [](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/8912?t=3&akid=8234%2E868730%2En0XvBe) Friend, What we saw on Wednesday was not a protest -- it was an insurrection. Our nation's tensions have been bubbling to this for four long years, and Wednesday, they finally boiled over. Americans need to know that this was not confined to just the capital of our nation -- credible threats to the health and safety of our public servants have been happening for months. In Fulton County, Georgia, officials had to stop counting votes and leave for safety reasons. In Michigan, a group of Trump supporters were thwarted in their attempts to kidnap the governor. And right here in Washington, an angry mob forced their way into the grounds of the Governor's Residence and threatened journalists at our state Capitol. **Every day Trump stays in office puts the American people in very real danger. That's why I've joined Senator Patty Murray and six members of the Washington state congressional delegation to call for Donald Trump's removal from office by any legal means necessary.** It is clear that he is unfit for office and could easily put our democracy at risk again before Joe Biden's January 20th inauguration. We cannot afford to wait. **[I spoke about my reactions and emotions in light of Wednesday's events in a video message -- I'd love it if you could watch, share, and spread the message that we must seek common ground even in the most difficult of times, including right now.](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/14096?t=4&akid=8234%2E868730%2En0XvBe)** I'm reminded of President Lincoln's words during another moment of national tension in American history: _"Though passion may have strained, it must not break our bonds of affection. The mystic chords of memory, stretching from every battle-field, and patriot grave, to every living heart and hearthstone, all over this broad land, will yet swell the chorus of the Union, when again touched, as surely they will be, by the better angels of our nature."_ Be well and stay safe -- despite Wednesday's violence, I still believe brighter days are ahead of us. Very truly yours, Jay | [WATCH AND SHARE](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/14096?t=5&akid=8234%2E868730%2En0XvBe)
---
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](http://act.jayinslee.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=6&akid=8234%2E868730%2En0XvBe). Contributions or gifts to JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D) are not tax deductible. | PAID FOR BY JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D)
PO BOX 21067 SEATTLE, WA 98111
---
All content (C) 2020 JAY INSLEE FOR WASHINGTON (D), All Rights Reserved
[Privacy Policy](https://act.jayinslee.com/go/3?t=2&akid=8234%2E868730%2En0XvBe)

Our nation's tensions have been bubbling to this for four long years, and Wednesday, they finally boiled over. Americans need to know that this was not confined to just the capital of our nation -- credible threats to the health and safety of our public servants have been happening for months. In Fulton County, Georgia, officials had to stop counting votes and leave for safety reasons. In Michigan, a group of Trump supporters were thwarted in their attempts to kidnap the governor. And right here in Washington, an angry mob forced their way into the grounds of the Governor's Residence and threatened journalists at our state Capitol. Every day Trump stays in office puts the American people in very real danger. That's why I've joined Senator Patty Murray and six members of the Washington state congressional delegation to call for Donald Trump's removal from office by any legal means necessary. It is clear that he is unfit for office and could easily put our democracy at risk again before Joe Biden's January 20th inauguration. We cannot afford to wait. I'm reminded of President Lincoln's words during another moment of national tension in American history: "Though passion may have strained, it must not break our bonds of affection. The mystic chords of memory, stretching from every battle-field, and patriot grave, to every living heart and hearthstone, all over this broad land, will yet swell the chorus of the Union, when again touched, as surely they will be, by the better angels of our nature." Be well and stay safe -- despite Wednesday's violence, I still believe brighter days are ahead of us. Very truly yours, Jay |

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Urgent Senate Update: How We Move Forward
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501148825%2501100508%2b-%2b01-09-2021%2beNewsletter%250115439.23217893.5019741.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501148825%2501100508%2b-%2b01-09-2021%2beNewsletter%250115439.23217893.5019741.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **January 9, 2021** |
---|---|---|---
| | | Early Thursday morning, our democracy prevailed. Despite President Trump's calls for insurrection, his contempt for the Constitution, and his lawless conduct, the will of the American people held firm. The historic victory of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris has been certified by the United States Congress and they will both be sworn in as President and Vice President on January 20th. But this resounding testament to the durability of our institutions was interrupted earlier on Wednesday by a violent and seditious attack on our republic. I never thought I would witness this type of assault on our Capitol and our democracy - both from without and within. The President ignited this fire and the complicity of far too many Republican members of Congress fueled the flames. Following the attacks, I [spoke](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-speaks-on-senate-floor-against-dangerous-and-unfounded-republican-attempts-to-block-electoral-college-vote-certification-mob-violence-at-us-capitol&redir_log=32796241261484) on the Senate floor about Republican lawmakers' irresponsible and unfounded attempts to challenge November's election results, and the damaging consequences of their actions. President Trump instigated this attack and must be removed from office. Each day he remains in the White House is a threat to our country. While only a few days still remain in his term, Trump is a ticking time bomb aimed at the heart of our democracy. Washington's leaders have a moral responsibility to intervene before the fuse blows. I have already [urged](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fposts%2f10158243275263878&redir_log=547089483921395) Vice President Pence to invoke the 25th Amendment. Should he and the Cabinet fail to act, I believe that Congress must move forward with articles of impeachment. This has been a dark week for our democracy, but there is still cause for hope. On Tuesday, the people of Georgia raised their voices and sent two new leaders to Washington, D.C. - Rev. Raphael Warnock, the senior pastor at the historic Ebenezer Baptist Church where Dr. King once held the pulpit, and Jon Ossoff. Their victories have given the Democrats a razor-thin majority in the Senate and, importantly, taken the gavel out of the hands of Senator McConnell. We will move with urgency to defeat the raging COVID-19 pandemic, deliver more emergency relief to struggling Marylanders, build an economy that works for all, tackle the deeply-rooted problems of racial and social inequality, and work to mend the torn fabric of our democracy. In his second inaugural address, Abraham Lincoln issued a now famous call to "bind up the nation's wounds." Despite the wake of blood, betrayal, and violence left by the Civil War, Lincoln determined that the only way forward was in peace and unity. In only a few days, we will inaugurate a new President and Vice President who will be tasked with helping a divided country come together again. Joe Biden and Kamala Harris have promised to serve both those who voted for them and those who didn't. I know that they will uphold this pledge. It remains to be seen whether a substantial number of Senate Republicans will accept their hand of unity. I do know that, [with your input and help](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontact%2femail&redir_log=057510262874326), I will fight every day to help heal a broken nation, meet the urgent challenges before us, and set us on a brighter course. Chris Van Hollen
---
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fvideos%2f164518892096207&redir_log=281705650278393)
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284 "Facebook")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241 "Twitter")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292 "Instagram")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326 "YouTube")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)
|
---
[Please click here to unsubscribe](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//EsurveyForm.aspx?__cid=SenVanHollen&__sid=100007&__crop=15439.23217893.5019741.7517223)

NRCC is happy to help at 888-606-1023. Reply HELP for help, STOP to end. MessFreqMayVary. Msg&DataRatesMayApply. Message frequency may vary. name: Rep. Dana Criswell
subject: From Your Capitol – Week of January 5, 2021
body:
From Your Capitol – Week of January 5, 2021
---
| | |
---
| | [](https://4gzmh.r.ag.d.sendibm3.com/mk/cl/f/rc65GagHydlLUCCCgBBlcLYuK7BlMws41N_7lZrI0HLFDSxVeuZb68kHJ5NkhffPJYTcZ9DqK_O22VCsWig3bc1CE4nsMpwWCx2pr_aGE1qRk9egAdS9In5EltSGR_v77HDwE1HnSZuG8OyXLkfpApKN7OVWTRyul39fSkjlBbSnjg32KnFBRWjAvaxL5Ngu5aF7_YvrMA-m0bGDbCoR5DiGNb4RduKqcsDh9caPdBHFHW0SSIeriMOUwepflbNvRINCD3Wb1g)
---
| | | |
---
**From Your Capitol – Week of January 5, 2021**
| |
---
| | | |
---
Kayla Nations visited the capitol this week. Our freedoms are secure when citizens like Kayla stay involved.
| |
---
| | On Tuesday, January 5 the Mississippi State Legislature began its 133rd legislative session. The first order of business was to adopt the new state flag that was approved by more than seventy percent of voters on the November ballot. Many of us know that the way this new flag was adopted was in no way inclusive, right or fair. Rules were broken and the will of the people was ignored while legislators were threatened and coerced. But more than 70 percent of the people voted to adopt this flag and move on to other issues. The remainder of the first week of this session saw very little activity as legislators finalized bills and prepared for the deadline to submit legislation for consideration. Once this deadline has been reached, committees will begin meeting to consider the merits of each bill. Next Wednesday, January 13, is the deadline to request legislation, and Monday, January 18, is the deadline for filing bills. More than 190 House bills have already been filed and referred to committees. As our nation enters a time where our democracy is under attack and our newly elected federal government is marching us directly into socialism. It is imperative that the citizens of Mississippi elect local and state leaders who are willing to fight. Due to COVID-19, visitors to the Capitol are limited this year. All guided tours have been suspended, and the Capitol Gift Shop is closed. However, Mississippians are allowed to visit the Capitol as long as they adhere to the proper guidelines. **My Votes this Week**
---
| |
---
| | | [**Cick Here to see my votes for this week** ](https://4gzmh.r.ag.d.sendibm3.com/mk/cl/f/WphALhW1wqaJNziaqd8_iTVSaw8mUQ72cYQoYnlUlhemAqMxqFgw_j8b9OGeZX3gweTAgKxXLxazCIHv-mwOFnCoYvDo7TPa-xhX0-KKw0u1vPtz1lGJb7ho3kdwkxcu2Ur1fEg6kR6RZeRyT_ML-k3bx6gsr3V9YYrr8kbFiueASS31gm0b1OUnzuLogRw_-AJqWI5zzn181BX8Ssgoh9T5LDXzw9yRJZV9DQ)

name: =?utf-8?Q?David=20Perdue?=
subject: Thank You
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/83693b08b7cc/tickets-ga-gop-defend-the-majority-rally-with-vice-president-pence-for-senators-david-perdue-and-kelly-loeffler-67583?e=02cd889ad3)
---
|
---
|
---
|
> Friends, When Bonnie and I first ran for the United States Senate in 2014, we did so because we saw two crises: a financial crisis and a global security crisis. We believed that we could make a difference and help change the direction of our country. With your support and your prayers, I believe we did just that. In these last six years, we helped lead the greatest economic turnaround in American history that helped 6.5 million Americans pull themselves out of poverty. We rebuilt our military. We reengaged with our allies around the world. We stood up to China. We defeated ISIS. Finally, we created a pathway for peace and stability in the Middle East. Right here in Georgia, our team delivered every day. We stood up for our farmers, got critical relief after Hurricane Michael, led the fight to finally deepen and expand the Port of Savannah, and fought every day to fully fund our military installations and the women and men in uniform and their families who all make the Peach State proud. At the outset of the unprecedented COVID-19 crisis, we moved quickly to bring historic relief to the state and saved 1.5 million Georgia jobs. Most importantly, we never stopped fighting for additional assistance and delivered it to the families, communities, and small businesses who needed it most. Now even more relief is on the way. I am very proud of what we accomplished together. Team Perdue served this state – all eleven million Georgians – the right way and we always did it for the right reasons. I am extremely grateful for how our team in Georgia and Washington, D.C. fought every day to deliver real results for all Georgians – no matter their political party. Our constituent services team exceeded every expectation and improved the lives of thousands of veterans, families, and everyday Georgians, who in many cases had nowhere else to turn. After winning the general election but missing the 50% threshold by just a few thousand votes, it now appears that we have come up short in this runoff election. Yesterday, I congratulated my opponent and the Democratic Party for their victory in this runoff. This was an exceptionally close election – we knew it would be. And while the results are deeply disappointing, our campaign was powered by the people of Georgia and the ideas that helped improve the lives of our neighbors. This was about a cause far greater than ourselves. This was never about Bonnie or me, it was about future generations. It still is, and that's why Bonnie and I will never stop fighting.
> To all those in this campaign who knocked a door, called a fellow Georgian, chipped in what they could, put up a yard sign, or supported our statewide bus tour, please know that you played a critical role in the most robust get-out-the-vote campaign in our state's history. It means more to Bonnie and me than you'll ever know. There aren't words to express my true thanks. I'll never forget what you've done for us, and for Georgia. We will be forever grateful for the friendships we’ve made along this journey, and we know our work for the people of Georgia is far from over. We look forward to seeing you all again soon. We left it all on the field. Keep your head held high. The fight for our state and our country isn't over. Thank you and God bless you. David
---
|
---
| | | | | | | [](https://perduesenate.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ebb829fcdaef555ddec2107e&id=6514ce49c0&e=02cd889ad3)
